data.body: Caligula did not cause the fall of Rome. While his erratic, four-year reign (37–41 AD) was marked by political turmoil, financial extravagance, and terror, occurred centuries before the Western Roman Empire fell. His death actually showed Rome’s resilience, as institutions survived despite his rule.
